_meta.exercise: NDSA GovID 2.0 Detection Validation — INCD-CID Section 8
_meta.authorization: INCD pre-notification filed 2026-02-10; CyberShield Ltd. engagement letter signed
_meta.lab_environment: NDSA GOVNET OPS replica (GovID 2.0 lab, VRID-SRV-01 lab, JUMPHOST lab)
_meta.red_team: CyberShield Ltd. + NDSA Detection Engineering (Gila Ben-Moshe)

results.test_action: Connected to lab PAM VPN gateway with contractor credentials at 02:00 IST (UTC+3)
results.finding: Alert fires but with 30-minute suppression window still active from prior FP-reduction config. First alert delayed. Without suppression: PASS. Must remove suppression and add country-anomaly condition before production deployment.
results.recommendation: Remove suppression window; add 'source country != IL' as additional condition to reduce FP while maintaining sensitivity.

results.test_action: SSH from contractor DMZ to GOVNET OPS (lab) on non-authorized port
results.finding: Alert fires correctly but with 30-minute delay due to NetFlow collection interval. In the March 2025 incident, adversary completed database dump in 35 minutes. A 30-minute detection delay means only 5 minutes of response time. Delay must be reduced.
results.recommendation: Reduce NetFlow collection interval from 30 min to 5 min for DMZ->GOVNET flows. Emergency change request submitted.

results.finding: Alert fires within 25 seconds. Lab confirmed bitsadmin.exe /transfer to external IP triggers rule. Note: rule not deployed on GovID 2.0 nodes — Windows-only; GovID 2.0 frontend runs on Linux.
results.recommendation: Deploy rule to all remaining Windows hosts. For Linux GovID 2.0 nodes, create equivalent rule using curl/wget monitoring.

results.finding: Fires within 2 minutes as documented. Highest-confidence rule in inventory. No tuning needed.

results.test_action: SELECT * FROM vrid_test_citizens (lab replica with 50k test records)
results.finding: Alert fires. Confirmed working. Lab replica uses same detection logic as production. Note: only covers VRID-SRV-01 schema — GovID 2.0 API logs not covered (different log format).
results.recommendation: Extend similar logic to GovID 2.0 API logs when Sprint 23 is deployed.

results.test_action: Sent 500 biometric match requests in 5 minutes via mock API client
results.finding: Rule is DRAFT and not deployed. Sprint 23 API structured logging not yet in lab environment. Mock test using manually crafted log entries confirms rule logic is correct, but end-to-end testing requires Sprint 23 completion.
results.recommendation: Schedule validation test for Sprint 24 (2 weeks after Sprint 23 deployment). Deploy to production only after passing lab test.

results.test_action: Sent 1000 authentication requests in 60 seconds to lab GovID API
results.finding: Rule is DRAFT and not deployed. API rate limit events require structured logging from Kong API Gateway — not yet configured in lab or production.
results.recommendation: Configure Kong rate-limit plugin to emit structured events to Elastic. Estimated: 3 days engineering effort. Schedule in next sprint.

results.test_action: Attempted to use staging API token against production endpoint in lab
results.finding: Sprint 23 fix not deployed — staging and production API namespaces not yet separated in lab. Rule logic is sound but cannot be tested until Sprint 23.
results.recommendation: Same Sprint 23 dependency as GOV-DET-006. Test in Sprint 24.

results.rule_name: AiTM phishing lookalike domain DNS resolution
results.test_action: DNS query for govid-authentication.com from lab contractor DMZ endpoint
results.finding: Rule is DRAFT. Sysmon not deployed on JUMPHOST-CONTRACTOR-01 (lab or production). DNS monitoring not available on Contractor DMZ. Cannot test without deploying DNS monitoring.
results.recommendation: Submit CAB change request to deploy Sysmon on JUMPHOST-CONTRACTOR-01. 5 business day approval. Compensating control: block known domains at DNS resolver immediately.

results.finding: Alert fires correctly. However, production alert-fatigue issue confirmed — Veeam backup triggers 3 alerts per night; SOC has learned to dismiss without investigation. Backup filter not functioning correctly for Veeam agent path.
results.recommendation: Fix filter_backup_tools: add Veeam service account (svc-veeam) to User exclusion list. Validate Veeam no longer triggers after fix.

summary.incd_compliance_assessment: SECTION 8 REQUIREMENT NOT MET — 50% pass rate on active rules; 4 rules not testable due to architectural blockers. INCD report must document gaps and remediation timeline.
critical_blockers: Sprint 23 not deployed — blocks GOV-DET-006, GOV-DET-007, GOV-DET-008
No DNS monitoring on Contractor DMZ — blocks GOV-DET-009
summary.board_presentation_recommendation: Do NOT claim full detection coverage. Report: 3 rules confirmed working, 3 rules partially working with known fixes, 4 rules blocked pending architectural changes. Provide remediation timeline.
